Service Education

Exterior vs Interior Window Cleaning: What Each Service Includes and When You Need Both

Published 2026-05-23 · Wagon Windows

Exterior window cleaning and full interior-exterior window cleaning sound like simple variations on the same service—but they're distinct processes requiring different tools, different access logistics, and different time investments. Understanding what each service includes helps you choose the right option for your home, your schedule, and your budget.

Need professional window cleaning in the Shuswap?

Book Online — Free Quote (403) 837-5092

What Exterior Window Cleaning Includes

Exterior window cleaning addresses the outside face of every accessible window pane on the home. It's the more commonly booked service for a simple reason: the exterior faces are by far the dirtier surfaces. Weather, dust, pollen, bird activity, spider webs, and—in the Shuswap—mineral deposits from lake spray and irrigation all accumulate on exterior glass. Interior surfaces, by contrast, accumulate primarily household dust, occasional fingerprints, and condensation residue. A thorough exterior cleaning includes: removal of any loose debris, cobwebs, and dry contaminants from the glass surface before solution is applied (dry brushing); application of professional cleaning solution via T-bar applicator; squeegee removal with the single-pass technique; edge detailing with a lint-free cloth; and a final wipe of the exterior windowsill and frame surround. Screen removal before exterior cleaning and reinstallation after is standard—screens left in place block direct access to the glass and produce inferior results. When screens are removed as part of the service, they're typically shaken or brushed to remove loose debris before reinstallation. Full screen cleaning is a separate service from this light removal-and-reinstall step. Exterior cleaning also addresses frame condition in a functional sense: the squeegee and sill wipe process removes surface debris from frames, though it doesn't include deep track cleaning (a separate service) or pressure washing of frames. For most residential properties, exterior cleaning is the most impactful single service. The visual difference is dramatic—dirty exterior glass makes the whole home look neglected; clean exterior glass has an immediate curb appeal effect that interior cleaning alone cannot achieve.

What Interior Window Cleaning Adds

Interior window cleaning addresses the inside face of each window pane. When booked as a full service alongside exterior cleaning, the cleaner works both faces of each window in sequence—typically exterior first, then interior, so any light exterior residue doesn't contaminate the interior glass on the return pass. The interior process is similar in technique—T-bar applicator, squeegee, edge detailing—but differs in access logistics and tool management. Interior cleaning requires moving furniture or objects near windows, working in occupied living spaces, and managing water more carefully to prevent floor or surface contact. Professional cleaners work with cloths to catch drips immediately and take care around soft furnishings, electronics, and any surfaces near the window. Interior cleaning also addresses accumulated condensation residue and film that forms on interior glass over time. In kitchens and bathrooms, cooking vapour and steam contribute to a greasy film that doesn't exist on exterior glass. In living areas, dust mixed with outgassing from furnishings creates a subtle haze that's most visible in oblique light. These interior-specific deposits require the same squeegee technique as exterior cleaning but may need a slightly more concentrated solution or a second application for heavier films. Interior cleaning typically adds 30–60 minutes to a full-home service, depending on window count and access complexity. It's priced in addition to exterior cleaning—ask about the combined price when booking.

  • Interior service adds access logistics: furniture movement, floor protection, drip management
  • Kitchen and bathroom interior glass develops grease and steam film not present on exteriors
  • Living area glass develops dust-and-outgassing haze visible in oblique light
  • Adds 30–60 minutes to a full-home service; priced in addition to exterior

When Exterior-Only Makes Sense

For many homeowners and many scheduling situations, exterior-only cleaning is the right choice—not a compromise, but a deliberate and sensible selection. Exterior-only is appropriate when: interior windows are reasonably clean (regular household maintenance keeps interior glass in acceptable condition); the primary motivation is curb appeal or pre-event preparation; scheduling requires minimal home access or occupation time; or budget requires prioritizing the highest-impact service. Because exterior glass accumulates far more contamination than interior glass, exterior cleaning produces proportionally more visual impact per dollar spent. A home with clean exterior glass and slightly dusty interior glass looks significantly cleaner than the reverse—the exterior face is what catches light from outside and what guests and passersby see. Exterior-only is also appropriate for homeowners who prefer to clean interior windows themselves or who have pets or small children making coordinating interior access logistically complex. The service can be completed efficiently with no one home, while interior cleaning typically benefits from the homeowner being present to move objects near windows. For pre-sale preparation, the decision depends on buyer demographic and showing conditions. In strong seller's markets, exterior-only usually covers the visual requirement. In competitive markets where buyers scrutinize thoroughly, full interior-exterior cleaning ensures no detail reads as neglected. Wagon Windows offers exterior-only and full interior-exterior packages. We'll recommend what makes sense for your situation when you call—not upsell for the sake of it.

How the Two Services Differ in Execution

Beyond scope, exterior and interior window cleaning differ in the tools used, the cleaning solutions appropriate, and the care required during execution. Exterior cleaning is physically active work—cleaners move around the building, use extension poles and ladders for upper floors, manage buckets and equipment on outdoor surfaces, and work in weather conditions (wind, direct sun, temperature variations). The work is efficient because access is relatively uncomplicated and drips and water contact with exterior surfaces are generally not a concern. Interior cleaning requires more careful management. Solution application must account for walls, floors, and furnishings within splatter range. Squeegee pulls must be controlled to prevent water from running off the sill onto interior surfaces. Drop cloths or towels are positioned at the sill line to catch any water. Cleaners working indoors are also more careful about footwear, equipment placement, and communication with the homeowner about what needs to be moved. The cleaning solution used may differ slightly. Interior glass that carries cooking film or condensation residue may benefit from a slightly higher surfactant concentration than standard exterior cleaning solution. Ammonia-based solutions are sometimes used for interior kitchen glass where grease film is present—but not on tinted or coated glass, where ammonia can cause discolouration. Professionally, the two services are often performed by the same cleaner on the same visit, moving systematically through the home. For large homes with many windows, some companies use two-person teams—one cleaner working exterior, one interior—to reduce total visit time.

Which Service Is More Popular and Why

In the Shuswap residential market, exterior-only cleaning is the more commonly booked service, and the reasons are practical. First, exterior glass is the dirtier surface by a significant margin. Shuswap-area homes face weather exposure, pollen, cottonwood, and mineral deposits from lake and irrigation water. Exterior glass can go from professionally clean to visibly dirty in a single week during peak cottonwood or following a rain event that leaves mineral deposits. Interior glass, by comparison, accumulates slowly. Second, exterior cleaning requires less homeowner coordination. There's no need to be home, no need to move furniture, and no concern about cleaners in occupied living spaces. For busy homeowners or properties that are seasonally occupied (many Shuswap lake properties are), the ability to book exterior cleaning without being present is a meaningful convenience. Third, exterior cleaning produces the visible impact that motivates the decision to book in the first place. Homeowners notice dirty exterior glass from the driveway. The improvement from a professional exterior clean is immediately visible and satisfying in a way that interior cleaning—which addresses subtler accumulation—is not. Full interior-exterior cleaning is more common for pre-sale preparation, post-construction cleaning, spring deep-cleaning projects, and clients who want comprehensive service on a seasonal schedule. These homeowners value the complete result over the convenience of exterior-only access. Wagon Windows books both services regularly across Salmon Arm and the Shuswap. Call (403) 837-5092 to discuss what's right for your home and timeline. Residential exterior starts at $169.

Frequently Asked Questions

What's the difference between exterior and interior window cleaning?

Exterior window cleaning addresses the outside face of every accessible pane—the dirtiest surface, exposed to weather, pollen, and mineral deposits. Interior cleaning addresses the inside face, which accumulates household dust, condensation residue, and cooking film more slowly. Full service covers both; exterior-only is the more commonly booked option and produces most of the visible impact.

Do I need to be home for exterior window cleaning?

Generally no. Exterior cleaning is conducted entirely outside the home and doesn't require interior access. Cleaners work around the building perimeter and can complete the service without the homeowner present. Interior cleaning does benefit from the homeowner being home to facilitate object movement near windows, though this can be arranged in advance.

How much more does interior window cleaning cost compared to exterior-only?

Interior cleaning adds 30–50% to a typical residential exterior cleaning price, depending on home size and access complexity. The additional cost reflects the added time, care, and logistics of working indoors. Wagon Windows prices the combined service transparently—call (403) 837-5092 for a quote that covers both.

Does Wagon Windows clean both interior and exterior windows?

Yes. We offer exterior-only and full interior-exterior residential cleaning. Exterior cleaning starts at $169. Full interior-exterior cleaning is quoted based on home size and window count. We recommend the right service for your situation—we don't push interior if exterior alone serves your needs.
